suite.name: Developer Daily Playbook Suite
suite.purpose: Compare baseline, TokenOpt-only, CodeGraph-only, TokenOpt+CodeGraph adaptive, and TokenOpt+CodeGraph hybrid on representative daily developer prompts: business deepdive, investigate-flow, pbi-plan, write-unittest-class, requirement-analysis, investigate-pbi, e2e-trace-flow, bug-trace, performance-analysis, security-audit, review-code, implement handoff, and refactor-code.

rows.finalAnswer: {"summary":"TokenOpt packet 908c34d5-6f4f-410a-87d7-052835414cea marked answerable=true, missing=[]. CodeGraph exact slices show recall is a due-list learning loop: backend loads due MemoryTracker items, frontend holds the current session/window state, answer endpoints persist attempts, and MemoryTracker/ForgettingCurve compute nextRecallAt from correctness, timing, delay, and spelling/non-spelling mode.","business_flow":["Due loading: GET /api/recalls/recalling takes timezone and optional dueindays, asserts login, gets current UTC, then calls RecallService.getDueMemoryTrackers for the current user (backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallsController.java:39-51).","Due list shape: RecallService maps due MemoryTracker rows into MemoryTrackerLite with memoryTrackerId and spelling, sets dueInDays, toRepeat, totalAssimilatedCount, and currentRecallWindowEndAt aligned to the user's half-day window (backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/RecallService.java:47-68).","Frontend session state: useRecallData stores toRepeat, currentRecallWindowEndAt, totalAssimilatedCount, currentIndex, treadmillMode, diligentMode, pause/resume flags, and computes toRepeatCount as remaining items after currentIndex (frontend/src/composables/useRecallData.ts:5-20,50-87).","Question loading: RecallPage tests mount the recall page with RecallsController.recalling, previouslyAnswered, and MemoryTrackerController.askAQuestion mocked, so the UI session is expected to load due trackers then ask per-tracker prompts (frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts:105-127,147-149).","Non-spelling answer path: POST /api/recall-prompts/{recallPrompt}/answer goes through answerQuiz with AnswerDTO, current user, and current timestamp into recallQuestionService.answerQuestion (backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptController.java:63-73). Tests show it validates correctness, increments recallCount, saves thinkingTimeMs, and changes the forgetting index depending on due timing and fast/slow answers (backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:85-146).","Spelling answer path: POST /api/recall-prompts/{recallPrompt}/answer-spelling uses AnswerSpellingDTO and read authorization on the prompt's MemoryTracker (backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptController.java:76-83). Tests show spelling accepts one of slash-separated titles, creates Answer.spellingAnswer, stores thinkingTimeMs/correct, forbids answering twice, and updates the MemoryTracker schedule like quiz answers (backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:394-476).","Scheduling: markAsRecalled increments recallCount, then recalledSuccessfully updates forgettingCurveIndex, lastRecalledAt, and nextRecallAt; recallFailed lowers the curve and schedules the next recall 12 hours later (backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/MemoryTracker.java:91-123). ForgettingCurve converts curve index to repeat hours, rewards/penalizes delay and thinkingTimeMs, clamps thinking time to 0-60000ms, uses 25000ms as neutral, and keeps the index at least 100 (backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/ForgettingCurve.java:19-70).","Treadmill mode: evidence shows treadmillMode is client-side recall-session state with setTreadmillMode and currentIndex/toRepeatCount; exact RecallPage.vue slices were unavailable, so its behavioral contract appears UI-level rather than a distinct backend scheduling path (frontend/src/composables/useRecallData.ts:10-20,50-87)."],"core_entities":["MemoryTracker: per-user note recall state; has spelling flag, forgettingCurveIndex, recallCount, lastRecalledAt, nextRecallAt, and markAsRecalled scheduling behavior.","ForgettingCurve: domain policy for spaced repetition interval, success/failure curve movement, delay sensitivity, and thinkingTimeMs adjustment.","DueMemoryTrackers: API payload containing dueInDays, toRepeat, totalAssimilatedCount, and currentRecallWindowEndAt for the current recall window.","MemoryTrackerLite: frontend due-list item with memoryTrackerId and spelling, letting the UI branch into spelling vs normal prompt flow.","RecallPrompt and Answer: per-question prompt and persisted answer result, including correct, thinkingTimeMs, and spellingAnswer for spelling prompts.","useRecallData state: shared frontend recall session metadata: toRepeat, currentIndex, toRepeatCount, currentRecallWindowEndAt, treadmillMode, diligentMode, pause/resume, and refresh nonce."],"files":["backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallsController.java:39-63 - recall loading and previously answered endpoints.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptController.java:63-83 - answerQuiz and answerSpelling API entrypoints.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/RecallService.java:39-68 - due tracker selection, toRepeat payload, total count, currentRecallWindowEndAt.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/MemoryTracker.java:91-123 - nextRecallAt calculation and success/failure scheduling.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/ForgettingCurve.java:5-72 - repeat interval, thinkingTimeMs adjustment, failure penalty.","frontend/src/composables/useRecallData.ts:5-87 - recall session state including toRepeat, currentRecallWindowEndAt, treadmillMode, currentIndex.","frontend/src/pages/RecallPage.vue - requested exact slices returned not found in CodeGraph index; behavior inferred only from tests/composable evidence.","frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts:1-149 - RecallPage imports, mocked SDK calls, useRecallData session-state test setup.","backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:64-170 - quiz answer correctness, thinkingTimeMs, curve tests.","backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:373-476 - spelling answer correctness, persistence, double-answer guard, curve tests."],"symbols":["recalling","answerQuiz","answerSpelling","getDueMemoryTrackers","getMemoryTrackersNeedToRepeat","markAsRecalled","recalledSuccessfully","recallFailed","calculateNextRecallAt/nextRecallAt","ForgettingCurve.succeeded","treadmillMode","toRepeat","currentRecallWindowEndAt","thinkingTimeMs","spelling","currentIndex/toRepeatCount"],"existing_tests":["frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts:105-127 mocks RecallsController.recalling, previouslyAnswered, MemoryTrackerController.askAQuestion, and useRecallData for RecallPage sessions.","frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts:51-99 models session fields including toRepeat, currentRecallWindowEndAt, totalAssimilatedCount, treadmillMode, currentIndex, diligentMode, refresh nonce.","backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:85-146 verifies answerQuiz correctness, recallCount update, thinkingTimeMs persistence, due-timing curve behavior, and fast vs slow answer scoring.","backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:394-448 verifies answerSpelling title matching, MemoryTracker update, Answer.spellingAnswer/thinkingTimeMs persistence, correctness, and no double answer.","backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java:451-476 verifies spelling scheduling/index behavior and fast-answer setup."],"risks":["frontend/src/pages/RecallPage.vue exact slices failed with file not found in CodeGraph index, so treadmill-mode behavior and page-level branching are not fully evidenced here.","RecallPromptController.java slice ends at line 83, so the rest of answerSpelling implementation and RecallQuestionService internals are not directly visible; tests provide outcome evidence.","thinkingTimeMs affects scheduling and is accepted from the client; ForgettingCurve clamps it, but UI collection/tamper behavior is not covered by the available slices.","currentRecallWindowEndAt is half-day/timezone aligned while dueindays shifts current UTC by 24h; product wording should avoid implying a simple calendar-day boundary.","No exact slice here shows incorrect-answer/failure tests for recallFailed; scheduling failure behavior is evidenced in MemoryTracker code but not test coverage in the selected ranges."],"next_questions":["What should treadmillMode promise: infinite practice, auto-loading future due items, repeating missed items, or only continuing UI navigation through toRepeat?","Should spelling answers use the same spaced-repetition scoring as MCQ answers despite different effort and typo risk?","Should currentRecallWindowEndAt be displayed as a half-day boundary, and should sessions refresh automatically when the window changes?","Are incorrect-answer and recallFailed paths covered in tests outside the selected ranges, especially for spelling?"]}
rows.prompt: Daily task: business deepdive the learner recall experience. Explain how due recall loading, answering questions, spelling vs non-spelling paths, scheduling, current recall window metadata, and treadmill mode fit together. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, business_flow, core_entities, files, symbols, existing_tests, risks, next_questions. Do not modify files.
rows.codexPrompt:
Daily task: business deepdive the learner recall experience. Explain how due recall loading, answering questions, spelling vs non-spelling paths, scheduling, current recall window metadata, and treadmill mode fit together. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, business_flow, core_entities, files, symbols, existing_tests, risks, next_questions. Do not modify files.

Benchmark constraints:
- Preserve the requested output format exactly.
- Cite repository-relative files when the task asks for citations.
- Do not modify files.
- Repository root: D:\Personal\Projects\doughnut
- Adaptive policy: tokenopt-codegraph-quality. Business/PBI/bug-trace prompts need backend, frontend, domain-state, and test evidence; use a bounded quality escalator instead of compact-only evidence.
- Use TokenOpt as the quality gate and CodeGraph as a bounded quality evidence escalator.
- First call tokenopt_compile_evidence with cwd=D:\Personal\Projects\doughnut, task_type=research_business, budget_tokens=1800, quality_rubric=["Connects backend recall loading, answer submission, scheduling state, and frontend session behavior.","Separates spelling and non-spelling paths and names current-window metadata.","Names existing source and test surfaces."], and task set to only the original Daily task text above.
- Treat the TokenOpt packet as a slot checklist, not as sufficient final evidence. Required slots: business_or_bug_goal, backend_entrypoint_api, service_domain_logic, frontend_state_or_caller_when_present, source_files, symbols, existing_tests, business_invariants_or_bug_symptom, validation_commands, risks.
- When calling any MCP tool, pass only the original Daily task/diff text, never this Benchmark constraints section or tool instructions.
- Then call CodeGraph get_file_slice with args={"slices":[{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallsController.java","lines":"20-63","maxChars":3600},{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptController.java","lines":"18-83","maxChars":3600},{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/RecallService.java","lines":"18-83","maxChars":4200},{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/MemoryTracker.java","lines":"80-123","maxChars":3600},{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/ForgettingCurve.java","lines":"1-120","maxChars":4200},{"file":"frontend/src/pages/RecallPage.vue","lines":"1-180","maxChars":5600},{"file":"frontend/src/pages/RecallPage.vue","lines":"240-430","maxChars":6200},{"file":"frontend/src/composables/useRecallData.ts","lines":"1-90","maxChars":3600},{"file":"frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts","lines":"1-220","maxChars":5200},{"file":"backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java","lines":"60-220","maxChars":5200},{"file":"backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java","lines":"372-575","maxChars":5200}]}.
- Use the exact slices as primary evidence. If a requested slice returns an error or one priority anchor is still absent, make at most one search_code call with query="RecallPromptController answerQuiz answerSpelling RecallPage.vue useRecallData ForgettingCurve recallFailed thinkingTimeMs", outputMode=compact, maxResponseTokens=6000, includeTests=true; otherwise make no generic ranked search.
- Carry exact CodeGraph evidence into the final answer.
- Priority anchors that should appear in the final JSON when present in the exact slices: RecallsController.java, RecallPromptController.java, RecallService.java, MemoryTracker.java, ForgettingCurve.java, RecallPage.vue, useRecallData.ts, RecallPage.spec.ts, recalling, answerQuiz, answerSpelling, getDueMemoryTrackers, markAsRecalled, recalledSuccessfully, recallFailed, treadmillMode, toRepeat, currentRecallWindowEndAt, nextRecallAt, thinkingTimeMs, spelling.
- Prefer exact slice evidence over ranked search results. Ignore unrelated CLI/E2E/Notebook hits unless the prompt explicitly asks for them.
- Do not omit priority anchors just to satisfy compact limits; priority anchors win over ancillary files and symbols.
- Quality hard gate: do not claim the task is fully covered unless the final JSON names at least one backend/API or service/domain file, relevant symbols, and existing tests when tests are requested by the prompt/rubric.
- For PBI/business prompts touching UI behavior, include frontend page/composable/state evidence when present; if it remains absent after the allowed calls, list it under unknowns/risks instead of inventing it.
- For bug-trace prompts, separate symptom path, likely root-cause method/state, focused regression tests, and fix handoff.
- Adaptive quality budget: at most 3 MCP calls total: 1 TokenOpt passport call, 1 exact batch CodeGraph get_file_slice call, and at most 1 search_code fallback only if a priority slice/anchor is missing. Shell fallback is disabled.
- Final ideas/proposals/tests must be evidence-grounded: each concrete idea should name relevant files or symbols, explain business/behavior value, include validation/tests, and state risks/tradeoffs.
- Final output must be a syntactically valid compact single JSON object under 7500 characters. Close every array/object. Prefer short evidence-rich strings over nested objects unless the user explicitly requested nested objects.
- Compact limits: max 5 existing_coverage items, max 12 files, max 16 symbols, max 5 tests_to_add, max 5 risks. Preserve all requested top-level keys exactly.
- If a slot remains missing, do not invent it; include it as a risk or missing_coverage item in the requested JSON shape.
- Preserve the requested JSON contract exactly.
qualityRubric: Connects backend recall loading, answer submission, scheduling state, and frontend session behavior.
Separates spelling and non-spelling paths and names current-window metadata.
Names existing source and test surfaces.

rows.finalAnswer: {"pbi_summary":"Add recall forecast counts so the Recall page can show due-now and next 3/7/14 day availability before learners click load-more. TokenOpt packet d3c08e14-d35e-4d17-9658-c9847d8648a1 marked answerable=true, missing=[]. Primary CodeGraph evidence covers backend API/service/DTO, frontend recall composable, and tests; exact RecallPage.vue slices were missing from the index.","business_flow":["Learner opens Recall page; current flow calls GET /api/recalls/recalling with timezone and optional dueindays, then displays/recalls returned toRepeat items.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallsController.java:39 maps /api/recalls/recalling, parses timezone, gets current UTC timestamp, and passes dueindays default 0 to RecallService.getDueMemoryTrackers.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/RecallService.java:39 computes the recall window by TimestampOperations.addHoursToTimestamp(currentUTCTimestamp,dueInDays*24) and delegates to userService.getMemoryTrackersNeedToRepeat(user,...,timeZone).","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/RecallService.java:47 returns DueMemoryTrackers with toRepeat, dueInDays, totalAssimilatedCount, and currentRecallWindowEndAt aligned by TimestampOperations.alignByHalfADay.","frontend/src/composables/useRecallData.ts:5 stores toRepeat/currentRecallWindowEndAt/totalAssimilatedCount/treadmillMode/currentIndex; toRepeatCount subtracts currentIndex, so forecast UI must not corrupt current-session progress."],"acceptance_criteria":["API response from RecallsController.recalling exposes forecast counts for windows 0,3,7,14 while preserving existing timezone and dueindays request compatibility.","Forecast computation reuses the same RecallService window semantics as current loading: currentUTCTimestamp plus dueInDays*24 and the same ZoneId/half-day alignment assumptions already tested in RecallsControllerTests.","Counts are monotonic by window; if UI says 'how many more', derive increments from cumulative counts without making the API windows non-monotonic.","Forecast counts exclude deleted/removed trackers using the same effective filtering as current getMemoryTrackersNeedToRepeat; do not rely on totalAssimilatedCount alone.","Existing Load more from next 3 days/7 days/14 days behavior remains compatible: returned toRepeat and dueInDays semantics are unchanged.","Recall page done state/buttons display forecast counts without resetting toRepeat,currentIndex,currentRecallWindowEndAt,treadmillMode,or dueRecallsRefreshNonce."],"impacted_files":["backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallsController.java:39-52 API entrypoint recalling(timezone,dueindays).","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/RecallService.java:39-68 domain loading/count window logic via getDueMemoryTrackers/getMemoryTrackersNeedToRepeat.","backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/dto/DueMemoryTrackers.java:9-14 DTO likely needs forecast field while keeping toRepeat/dueInDays/currentRecallWindowEndAt.","frontend/src/composables/useRecallData.ts:5-87 shared Recall page state; likely needs forecast storage/accessors if page state persists forecasts.","frontend/src/pages/RecallPage.vue requested by PBI, but CodeGraph exact slice returned file-not-found; treat as primary UI edit target to inspect next.","frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts:1-149 imports RecallPage and mocks useRecallData/RecallsController.recalling; existing slice covers test harness, not load-more assertions.","backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallsControllerTests.java:47-107 existing timezone and half-day controller tests to extend.","generated frontend backend API types/sdk may need refresh after DueMemoryTrackers DTO changes; exact generator path not verified."],"symbols":["RecallsController.recalling","RecallService.getDueMemoryTrackers","RecallService.getMemoryTrackersNeedToRepeat","DueMemoryTrackers","DueMemoryTrackers.toRepeat","DueMemoryTrackers.dueInDays","DueMemoryTrackers.currentRecallWindowEndAt","RecallService.totalAssimilatedCount","useRecallData","toRepeatCount","setCurrentRecallWindowEndAt","treadmillMode","currentIndex","dueRecallsRefreshNonce","RecallsControllerTests.shouldGetMemoryTrackersBasedOnTimezone","RecallsControllerTests.shouldSetCurrentRecallWindowEndAtAlignedByHalfADay"],"unknowns":["Exact frontend/src/pages/RecallPage.vue implementation, including loadMore/loadCurrentDueRecalls/button text, was not available from CodeGraph; search only confirmed test imports it.","Whether forecast payload should be named forecastCounts,dueForecastCounts,or similar is a design choice; existing DTO has no field for counts beyond totalAssimilatedCount.","The repository/userService predicate that excludes deleted/removed trackers is outside the exact slices, so removal filtering must be verified before implementation.","frontend/tests/pages/RecallPage.spec.ts was truncated after line 149, so existing load-more and treadmill-mode test coverage is not fully known.","Exact validation commands/package scripts were not verified because shell fallback was disabled; run targeted backend controller and frontend RecallPage tests once planning edits start."],"risks":["Timezone/half-day drift: forecast counts must use the same ZoneId,currentUTCTimestamp,dueInDays*24,and alignByHalfADay semantics as existing loading/tests, or users will see counts that disagree with loaded items.","Monotonicity semantics: cumulative 0/3/7/14 counts satisfy monotonic, but UI 'more' labels should subtract previous/current counts to avoid misleading learners.","Performance: counting four windows by materializing full tracker streams may duplicate the current toRepeat query cost; prefer count-capable repository/service paths if available.","Compatibility: changing toRepeat,dueInDays,currentRecallWindowEndAt,or RecallsController.recalling parameters could break existing Load more from next 3/7/14 days behavior.","Frontend state: adding forecast display must not mutate currentIndex/toRepeatCount or treadmillMode, because current-session progress depends on those refs in useRecallData.ts."],"next_steps":["Inspect RecallPage.vue directly before edits to locate loadCurrentDueRecalls/loadMore and done-state rendering; CodeGraph could not provide the exact page slice.","Plan backend DTO extension in DueMemoryTrackers.java and compute windows [0,3,7,14] in RecallService.getDueMemoryTrackers using one currentUTCTimestamp and ZoneId, keeping existing toRepeat for requested dueindays unchanged.","Add backend tests in RecallsControllerTests for forecast windows, monotonic counts, timezone/half-day boundary behavior, and removed/deleted tracker exclusion.","Update generated API types, then update useRecallData.ts/RecallPage.vue so done-state and Load more from next 3 days/7 days/14 days show forecast-derived counts while preserving treadmillMode/currentIndex progress.","Validate with targeted RecallsControllerTests and frontend RecallPage.spec.ts/Vitest coverage once edits begin."]}
rows.prompt: Daily task: investigate this PBI before planning edits. PBI: As a learner, I want the Recall page to show how many recall items are due now and how many more would be loaded from the next 3, 7, and 14 days, so that I can decide whether to continue recalling without blindly clicking each load-more button. Acceptance criteria: the recall API exposes forecast counts for due windows 0, 3, 7, and 14 days using the same timezone and half-day alignment rules as current recall loading; counts are monotonic by window and exclude deleted/removed trackers; existing Load more from next 3/7/14 days behavior remains compatible; Recall page done state/buttons display forecast counts without breaking treadmill mode or current-session progress. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: pbi_summary, business_flow, acceptance_criteria, impacted_files, symbols, unknowns, risks, next_steps. Do not modify files.

Preserves Load more and treadmill/current-session behavior as compatibility constraints.

rows.finalAnswer: {"summary":"TokenOpt returned answerable=false due missing concrete failing artifact, so this handoff is grounded in the requested CodeGraph slices. The backend contract is clear: wrong answers should flow to MemoryTracker.markAsRecalled(false), which calls recallFailed and sets nextRecallAt to currentUTCTimestamp+12h. Since that entity logic is present, the likely regression is in the quiz answer caller path before recallFailed is reached or before correct=false is passed.","reproduction_path":["Create a note, non-spelling MemoryTracker, and recall prompt; set a fixed currentUTCTimestamp.","POST /api/recall-prompts/{recallPrompt}/answer with an incorrect AnswerDTO.choiceIndex; entrypoint is backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptController.java:63-73.","Expected chain: RecallQuestionService.answerQuestion -> MemoryTrackerService.updateMemoryTrackerAfterAnsweringQuestion -> markAsRecalled -> MemoryTracker.markAsRecalled(false) -> recallFailed -> TimestampOperations.addHoursToTimestamp(currentUTCTimestamp,12).","Assert the prompt answer is incorrect, recallCount increments, and memoryTracker.nextRecallAt equals currentUTCTimestamp+12h.","Also reproduce spelling through /api/recall-prompts/{recallPrompt}/answer-spelling with a nonmatching AnswerSpellingDTO.spellingAnswer; entrypoint starts at RecallPromptController.java:76-83 and service logic starts at MemoryTrackerService.java:152-165."],"root_cause_hypotheses":["Most likely: RecallQuestionService.answerQuestion no longer updates the memory tracker when Answer.correct is false, or it calls MemoryTrackerService.updateMemoryTrackerAfterAnsweringQuestion only on success. Controller.answerQuiz delegates there at RecallPromptController.java:69-73.","Entity behavior appears correct: MemoryTracker.recallFailed sets nextRecallAt using TimestampOperations.addHoursToTimestamp(currentUTCTimestamp,12), and MemoryTracker.markAsRecalled routes successful=false to recallFailed at backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/MemoryTracker.java:100-123.","Service behavior appears intended for quiz: MemoryTrackerService.updateMemoryTrackerAfterAnsweringQuestion reads thinkingTimeMs from recallPrompt.getAnswer(), filters to non-spelling trackers, and calls markAsRecalled at backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/MemoryTrackerService.java:110-126; rows.prompt: Daily task: bug trace. A regression report says that after a learner answers a recall prompt incorrectly, the next retry is no longer scheduled about 12 hours later. Trace the likely code path and produce a fix handoff. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, reproduction_path, root_cause_hypotheses, files, symbols, tests_to_run, fix_plan, risks. Do not modify files.
rows.codexPrompt:
Daily task: bug trace. A regression report says that after a learner answers a recall prompt incorrectly, the next retry is no longer scheduled about 12 hours later. Trace the likely code path and produce a fix handoff. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, reproduction_path, root_cause_hypotheses, files, symbols, tests_to_run, fix_plan, risks. Do not modify files.

Benchmark constraints:
- Preserve the requested output format exactly.
- Cite repository-relative files when the task asks for citations.
- Do not modify files.
- Repository root: D:\Personal\Projects\doughnut
- Adaptive policy: tokenopt-codegraph-quality. Business/PBI/bug-trace prompts need backend, frontend, domain-state, and test evidence; use a bounded quality escalator instead of compact-only evidence.
- Use TokenOpt as the quality gate and CodeGraph as a bounded quality evidence escalator.
- First call tokenopt_compile_evidence with cwd=D:\Personal\Projects\doughnut, task_type=investigate, budget_tokens=1800, quality_rubric=["Starts from the bug symptom and traces controller/service/entity paths.","Identifies wrong-answer retry scheduling as distinct from success scheduling.","Names focused regression tests and risks."], and task set to only the original Daily task text above.
- Treat the TokenOpt packet as a slot checklist, not as sufficient final evidence. Required slots: business_or_bug_goal, backend_entrypoint_api, service_domain_logic, frontend_state_or_caller_when_present, source_files, symbols, existing_tests, business_invariants_or_bug_symptom, validation_commands, risks.
- When calling any MCP tool, pass only the original Daily task/diff text, never this Benchmark constraints section or tool instructions.
- Then call CodeGraph get_file_slice with args={"slices":[{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptController.java","lines":"18-83","maxChars":3600},{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/MemoryTrackerService.java","lines":"100-165","maxChars":4200},{"file":"backend/src/main/java/com/odde/doughnut/entities/MemoryTracker.java","lines":"80-123","maxChars":3600},{"file":"backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java","lines":"60-220","maxChars":5200},{"file":"backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/controllers/RecallPromptControllerTests.java","lines":"372-575","maxChars":5200},{"file":"backend/src/test/java/com/odde/doughnut/services/MemoryTrackerServiceTest.java","lines":"1-220","maxChars":4200}]}.
- Use the exact slices as primary evidence. If a requested slice returns an error or one priority anchor is still absent, make at most one search_code call with query="RecallPromptController answerQuiz answerSpelling recallFailed TimestampOperations.addHoursToTimestamp MemoryTrackerServiceTest", outputMode=compact, maxResponseTokens=6000, includeTests=true; otherwise make no generic ranked search.
- Carry exact CodeGraph evidence into the final answer.
- Priority anchors that should appear in the final JSON when present in the exact slices: RecallPromptController.java, MemoryTrackerService.java, MemoryTracker.java, RecallPromptControllerTests.java, MemoryTrackerServiceTest.java, answerQuiz, answerSpelling, recallFailed, markAsRecalled, TimestampOperations.addHoursToTimestamp, thinkingTimeMs, spelling.
- Prefer exact slice evidence over ranked search results. Ignore unrelated CLI/E2E/Notebook hits unless the prompt explicitly asks for them.
- Do not omit priority anchors just to satisfy compact limits; priority anchors win over ancillary files and symbols.
- Quality hard gate: do not claim the task is fully covered unless the final JSON names at least one backend/API or service/domain file, relevant symbols, and existing tests when tests are requested by the prompt/rubric.
- For PBI/business prompts touching UI behavior, include frontend page/composable/state evidence when present; if it remains absent after the allowed calls, list it under unknowns/risks instead of inventing it.
- For bug-trace prompts, separate symptom path, likely root-cause method/state, focused regression tests, and fix handoff.
- Adaptive quality budget: at most 3 MCP calls total: 1 TokenOpt passport call, 1 exact batch CodeGraph get_file_slice call, and at most 1 search_code fallback only if a priority slice/anchor is missing. Shell fallback is disabled.
- Final ideas/proposals/tests must be evidence-grounded: each concrete idea should name relevant files or symbols, explain business/behavior value, include validation/tests, and state risks/tradeoffs.
- Final output must be a syntactically valid compact single JSON object under 7500 characters. Close every array/object. Prefer short evidence-rich strings over nested objects unless the user explicitly requested nested objects.
- Compact limits: max 5 existing_coverage items, max 12 files, max 16 symbols, max 5 tests_to_add, max 5 risks. Preserve all requested top-level keys exactly.
- If a slot remains missing, do not invent it; include it as a risk or missing_coverage item in the requested JSON shape.
- Preserve the requested JSON contract exactly.
qualityRubric: Starts from the bug symptom and traces controller/service/entity paths.
Identifies wrong-answer retry scheduling as distinct from success scheduling.
Names focused regression tests and risks.
